purpose: Define the temporary-link handshake with mandatory scan closure, governance-folder attachment, repository-wide read visibility, and approval-receipt-gated cleanup outside governance/.

completion_invariants: completion_state is computed from scan presence and residue state
completed requires all lane-required scans, review.outcome.json, gate.approval.json, and residue_state=none

hard_rules: Central governance must not become a mandatory runtime hop for service execution.
Governance may be attached to the service only as the governance/ folder surface.
Repository-wide read scans are allowed during intake, review, hygiene, and release checks.
An active auth contract is required before any repository-wide scan begins.
Temporary links must be releasable without central stateful coordination.
Service-local logs remain owned by the service repository or service operator.
A temporary link may grant guidance, validation, or approval gates, but not central runtime custody.
A missing required scan leaves the link incomplete and blocks release.
Production links may not reach a completed state without a pre-release-scan.
Plan approval must exist before any apply execution is allowed.
Cleanup outside governance/ is allowed only when the approval receipt explicitly permits the target scope.
Cleanup outside governance/ requires evidence plus a rollback-linked receipt before release.
Temporary-link kits must use the fixed file set under governance/links/active/<link-id> with no extra root-level files.
Evidence payloads and review payloads referenced by the link kit must remain outside the kit folder in the fixed governance roots.
